expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: final quality pass preserves layering and executable wording
expectations.evidence: In result.md, the benchmark outcome explicitly states alignment with the case focus: “aligns with Phase 6 and explicitly covers the case focus: ‘final quality pass preserves layering and executable wording.’” It then supports this with Phase 6 contract evidence: Phase 6 is “final quality / layering ... pass,” layering is “required” with an explicit 1–6 hierarchy, and executable wording is enforced via “executable-step checks” and validators like “validate_executable_steps.”

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase6
expectations.evidence: result.md repeatedly maps evidence to Phase 6: it frames the benchmark as “Phase 6 contract focus,” cites “From skill_snapshot/SKILL.md (Phase 6)” with Phase 6 work/output/--post requirements, and cites “From skill_snapshot/reference.md (Phase 6 gate)” requiring the Phase 6 draft to pass final layering and executable-step checks. The stated outcome is “PASS (advisory) — ... aligns with Phase 6.”
